filename_note: NAMING ERROR — file was saved as S010 but scene is S011a. RENAME COMPLETED 2026-06-27: now IMG009_S011a_mine_tunnel_approved.png.

fix_m1_gate.human_review_note: FIX-M1 mandates human producer review of every output before production use. This QA report provides the automated pre-screening. Final approval authority is the human producer.

gate_closed_note: Gate is not 'closed' — any future regeneration of this image still requires full human review. This approval applies to attempt 1 only.

no_people.detail: Zero human forms visible anywhere in the image

no_silhouettes.detail: No human silhouettes in the tunnel passage or background. The vanishing point at the far end shows only tunnel structure and the distant light source.

no_human_shadows.detail: Shadows present are cast by structural wooden support timbers — rectangular/geometric shapes, not human-coded. No shadow reads as a person.

no_suffering_depiction.detail: Empty tunnel passage. No implied suffering, confinement of persons, or distress.

no_forced_labor_depiction.detail: The space evokes industrial atmosphere without depicting any labor activity or human presence.

no_horror_framing.detail: Dark and atmospheric but reads as 'austere industrial' — not horror movie. The wooden support timbers and structural elements create documentary authenticity. No supernatural elements.

empty_industrial_space.detail: Mine tunnel passage with wooden support beams. Visually correct for an uninhabited coal mine tunnel interior.

austere_industrial_atmosphere.detail: 'Oppressive atmosphere' was replaced with 'austere industrial' in PROMPT_008_SAFE — the image reflects this: factual, structural, not emotionally loaded.

respectful_documentary_tone.detail: The image shows an empty space. It evokes the memory of the mine without depicting any human experience within it.

visual_observations: Tunnel passage: CONFIRMED — rectangular tunnel extending into darkness with clear perspective vanishing point
Wooden support timbers: PRESENT — wooden beams running along ceiling and sides of tunnel. Authentic historical coal mine construction. These are structural, not human-shaped.
Stone/rock walls: PRESENT — dark rough-hewn rock walls on both sides
Stone/gravel floor: PRESENT — natural stone floor surface
Distant light source: PRESENT — small dim light visible at the far end of the tunnel. No human forms around or in front of the light.
Shadows: ALL STRUCTURAL — rectangular beam shadows only. No shadow reads as a person.
No people anywhere: CONFIRMED — full tunnel scan, zero human forms at all depths
No chains, tools, or labor objects: CONFIRMED
Atmosphere: Dark, austere, documentary. Not horror. The timber supports give authentic industrial character.
Color: Near-monochrome dark grey-brown. Correctly desaturated.
naming_action_required: COMPLETED — File renamed from IMG009_S010_mine_gate_attempt1.png to IMG009_S011a_mine_tunnel_approved.png on 2026-06-27. The S010 in the original filename was a user entry error — scene is S011a.
post_process_notes: Minimal processing. Film grain. Palette is already near-monochrome dark — do not brighten. Apply slow_zoom_in at 30-second duration (S011a timecode 4:10–4:40). Narration L013/L014 — about forced labor memory — will play over this empty tunnel. The emptiness IS the statement.
